reasoning:
The reference 30-day average for the prior year in the Philippines is 4.833 events (translating to ~58 events annually). For typical, conflict-related time series, event counts are overdispersed (bursty), which means the median monthly count is often slightly below the mean unless there is a seasonal or systemic spike. This suggests a baseline probability of slightly below 50% for any given random 30-day period.

However, the Philippines will hold major nationwide and local elections on May 12, 2025. ACLED's 'Strategic developments' category includes "disrupted weapons use" (e.g., confiscating arms caches or Election Gun Ban violations by armed groups), "high-level arrests" (e.g., of politicians or private armed group leaders), and "looting/property destruction" (e.g., destruction of election materials). This indicates that the 30-day periods overlapping with the height of the campaign (mid-March through mid-May) and its immediate aftermath are highly likely to see above-average strategic development counts.

Thus, probabilities are elevated for the March-to-June 2025 resolution dates, peaking around the peak campaign period (evaluating Mar 15-Apr 14), then trailing off. By September 2025, the baseline returns to post-election normalcy, dropping the probability slightly below 50% due to the bursty nature of the series (median < mean). For March 2026, the forecast is set just below 0.5 as horizon uncertainty maximizes.
